biography.value:
Deputy Freja is the junior Chainwarden at Stairwatch’s post near the Ever-Ember. Young, sharp-eyed, and relentlessly suspicious. She does not drink — not on duty, not off it — and considers this a professional virtue rather than a personal preference.

Freja is by-the-book in the way people become when they have decided that rules are the only reliable thing in an unreliable world. She fills out forms meticulously, cross-references names against wanted notices, and notices when papers look a little too clean or a little too worn.

On Lew Tosferd: "He knows too many people. Nobody knows that many people honestly."
biography.director:
Freja appears in Beat 5 at the Chainwarden Post alongside Tormund. She is the difficult one in the duo: Tormund will wave people through; Freja will not.

Key interactions:

  • Will carefully inspect any papers the PCs produce. A Reason roll (Moderate, TN 12) to fabricate or present convincingly. She cross-checks against her ledger of recent transit notices.
  • Cannot be charmed over a drink or via social ease — Presence rolls have a +2 TN penalty with her specifically. She interprets friendliness as a manipulation tactic.
  • Can be won over with evidence: if the PCs cite something verifiable (a letter with a real seal, a name she can check), her Reason score works in their favour rather than against them.
  • Suspicious of Lew: she will warn the PCs that “someone who knows too many people” is not always reliable. This is concern, not malice — she genuinely thinks something is off about him.
  • Option A (Mine): Freja knows exactly which miners went in and when. She filed the missing-persons report herself. She will cooperate fully if the PCs commit to investigating — this is official and therefore something she understands.

Tone: Tightly wound, not unkind. She’s not obstructing the PCs out of spite — she’s doing her job in a town where the rules are the only thing still standing.
